data.title: Nxu shares jump on proposed merger with Verde Bioresins
data.description:
• Nxu (NASDAQ:NXU) said on Thursday that it has entered into a merger agreement with Verde Bioresins, pursuant to which Nxu will acquire all the issued and outstanding common shares of Verde in an all-stock transaction.
  • Upon closing of the merger, the combined company is expected to be renamed “Verde Bioresins, Corp.” and list its common stock on Nasdaq under the new ticker symbol “VRDE.”
  • Upon the closing of the merger, assuming Nxu’s aggregate enterprise value is approximately $16.2 million, pre-Merger Verde stockholders will own approximately 95% of the combined company and pre-Merger Nxu stockholders will own approximately 5% of the combined company.
  • After closing of the merger, the board of directors of the combined company will consist of seven members, six of whom will be appointed by Verde and one of whom will be appointed by Nxu.
  • Verde management team will manage the business of the combined company.
  • Nxu (NXU) shares were up more than 180% during Thursday trading hours.

data.title: Gen Digital initiated with Overweight rating ahead of Q2 earnings: Wells Fargo
data.description:
• Gen Digital (NASDAQ:GEN) was initiated with an Overweight rating by Wells Fargo, one week ahead of the cybersecurity firm's second quarter fiscal 2025 financial report.
  • Wells Fargo also set a $35 price target on the stock.
  • The bank believes Gen Digital can continue to grow earnings per share in the mid-teens due in part to the surge of cyberattacks that have exposed billions of consumers' personal records and data.
  • "Consumers will need to increase spending on protection for devices, internet access and identity," Wells Fargo noted. "GEN has a large opportunity to convert customers that use free solutions, while also cross-selling additional solutions to their installed base."
  • Gen Digital shares have surged nearly 60% over the past year.
  • Gen Digital is slated to report its second quarter fiscal 2025 results on October 30. The consensus estimate calls for earnings per share of $0.54 on revenue of $971.6M.

data.title: Wall Street's S&P 500 index is broadening out in the second half of 2024
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets showcased that Wall Street’s S&P 500 index (SP500) is broadening out, as the support it receives from the Magnificent 7 club decreases. The benchmark gauge is hovering near all-time highs, though it suffered a selloff on Wednesday.

“An interesting development has occurred during the second half of 2024 thus far – these seven stocks have underperformed the rest of the index and is something we find reassuring since the S&P 500 has hit a series of new record highs during this timeframe without this segment of the market leading the way,” BMO Capital Markets stated in an investor note.

Furthermore, the financial firm highlighted a significant increase in participation levels, with 300 S&P 500 stocks outperforming the broader index—nearly twice the number from the prior 18 months. BMO added: “We believe these trends, should they continue as we expect, are an ideal environment for stock pickers.”

See two BMO charts below showcasing the performance of the Magnificent 7 names versus the rest of the S&P 500 along with how many more stocks are outperforming the benchmark index:

For investors and traders looking to track the performance of the accompanying S&P 500 index (SP500), here are some ETFs and mutual funds that may be of interest: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (SSO), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), and (VFFSX).

data.title: Acuity to acquire QSC for $1.21B
data.description:
• Acuity Brands (NYSE:AYI) on Thursday said it would acquire QSC for a purchase price of $1.21B, or $1.1B net of about $100M in present value of expected tax benefits.
  • QSC provides a cloud-manageable audio, video and control platform. The company delivered sales of approximately $535M for the twelve months ending August 31, 2024.
  • The deal is expected to be accretive to Acuity’s’ fiscal 2025 full-year adjusted diluted earnings per share.
  • Acuity Brands anticipate funding the transaction using $600M of term loan financing and the remainder with cash on the balance sheet.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the second-quarter of fiscal 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Optimism among retail investors falls in latest AAII survey
data.description:
Investors’ optimism about the short-term outlook for the stock market continued to decrease for the second consecutive week, showed the latest AAII Sentiment survey.

A total of 37.7% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism, compared to 45.5% and 49%, respectively for the last two weeks. This week’s figure has gone below the historical average of 37.5%.

The percentage of those surveyed who felt bearish also went up for the week ended Oct 23, with the figure standing at 29.9% versus 25.4% last week.

As the fresh earnings session picks up steam, investors are bracing themselves for a mixed quarter. A post earnings plunge, along with recent news for some high-profile companies including McDonald’s (MCD), Starbucks (SBUX) and GE Aerospace (GE) and Verizon (VZ) has taken a toll on technology and some consumer stocks. The rising anxiety of the upcoming election is also adding to the woes.

McDonald's has been linked to an E. coli outbreak by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention that has sickened 49 people across ten states and killed one person. Similarly, Starbucks fell sharply in postmarket trading on Tuesday after reporting preliminary financial results for FQ4.

Neutral sentiment stood at 32.4% for the week, versus 29.2% last week, who saw no change in market direction.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) fell nearly 1% in the last one week, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) fell 1.6%. NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) fell 0.9% and NASDAQ 100-Index (NDX) fell 0.7%.

data.title: Dow's stock rises as Q3 sales beat estimates
data.description:
Dow’s (NYSE:DOW) stock rose 1.5% on Thursday morning after the chemical maker reported third-quarter results.

Adjusted earnings were $0.47 a share, in line with the consensus estimate.

Net sales rose 1% from a year earlier to $10.88 billion in the third quarter, led by higher sales in the United States and Canada. Analysts on average forecast sales of $10.68 billion.

The company last month had warned third-quarter sales would be less than previously forecast.

“Our cost-advantaged footprint in the Americas continues to provide a strong competitive edge, enabling Dow (DOW) to capture demand growth in attractive markets,” Jim Fitterling, chair and chief executive of Dow (DOW), said in a statement. “However, a meaningful recovery has yet to materialize in Europe and China.”

Sales grew 1.1% from the prior year in its packaging and specialty plastics business, and by 3.9% for its performance materials and coatings unit. However, sales fell 2.4% in its industrial and infrastructure unit.

data.title: TELUS collaborates with Google Cloud and Onix to modernize data storage
data.description:
• TELUS (NYSE:TU) said on Thursday that it has successfully completed its data-modernization journey in collaboration with Google Cloud and Onix.
  • The project overhauled and streamlined TELUS' data handling by moving from fragmented, on-premise sources to a unified, cloud-based platform – significantly improving data access and usability.
  • TELUS successfully upgraded hundreds of enterprise-grade data pipelines and migrated more than 14 petabytes of data from siloed legacy systems to Google Cloud over a three-year period.
  • This migration consolidated customer data from more than 100 data sources across TELUS into one central data hub, eliminating more than 30 percent of obsolete data stored on older, on-premise infrastructure.
